MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-24-2019 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH AND MODIFY SUBPEONAS ISSUED BY DEFENDANT

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY HOM'S MOTION TO QUASH AND MODIFY SUBPOENAS ISSUED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART; DENIED IN PART. THE SUBPOENAS TO THE MEDICAL PROVIDERS SHALL BE LIMITED TO 10 YEARS PRIOR TO THE INCIDENT. THE RECORDS SHALL BE FIRST PRODUCED TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FOR A "FIRST LOOK." IF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BELIEVES THAT ANY RECORDS ARE IRRELEVANT AND SHOULD NOT BE PRODUCED, THEY ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO ATTEMPT TO AGREE TO A NARROWER SCOPE. IF AN AGREEMENT CAN'T BE REACHED, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL SUBMIT THE RECORDS TO JPT MCDONALD FOR AN IN-CAMERA REVIEW. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY'S MOTION TO COMPEL MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. DR. STRASSBERG SHALL FOCUS PRIMARILY ON CONDUCTING THE PHYSICAL EXAMINATION BUT MAY ASK QUESTIONS THAT WILL ALLOW HIM TO FORM AN INTELLIGENT OPINION OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES. SEE SHARAFF V. SUPERIOR COURT (1955) 22 CAL.2D 508. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A "FULL MEDICAL HISTORY" IS NECESSARY FOR DR. STRASSBERG TO RENDER AN OPINION BUT HE SHALL BE ALLOWED TO ASK QUESTIONS ABOUT PLAINTIFF'S PAST MEDICAL CONDITIONS IF THEY DIRECTLY RELATE TO THE CONDITIONS AT ISSUE IN THE LITIGATION. THE CONDITIONS AT ISSUE ARE THOSE LISTED IN EXH. M TO THE GARRETT DECLARATION FILED IN SUPPORT OF THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL RESERVES THE RIGHT TO OBJECT TO DR. STRASSBERG'S QUESTIONS BUT SHALL NOT TERMINATE THE EXAMINATION AGAIN UNLESS THE QUESTIONING IS TRULY OPPRESSIVE. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Take Second Physical Examination Filed By Defendant San Francisco Zoological Society
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lauren Meisiel, Esq. 415-288-4037 of Meisel Krentsa & Burneikis for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court inquires re: status of the proposed stipulation to limit the testimony of Dr. Strassberg. Plaintiff reports to the Court. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY'S Motion For Leave To Take Second Physical Examination.

The Court modifies the tentative ruling. Defendant's request for a second physical examination of Plaintiff Kimberly Joy Hom by an orthopedist is granted conditioned upon the parties finalizing their stipulation of limiting the testimony of Dr. Strassberg.

Plaintiff to inform Defendant's Counsel of the modification and for Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Hearing Held, Plaintiff did not stipulate to have Judge Pro Tem to heard the matter. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY's Motion For Leave To Take Second Physical Examination Filed By Defendant San Francisco Zoological Society is continued to July 17, 2019 at 9:30am and parties should meet and confer prior to hearing regarding evidentiary stipulation. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Quash And Modify Subpoenas Issued By Defendant San Francisco Zoological Society, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lauren Meisel, esq. (415-788-2035) of Meisel, Krentsa & Burneikis for Plaintiff KIMBERLY HOM.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY HOM's Motion To Quash And Modify Subpoenas Issued By Defendant San Francisco Zoological Society, And Request For Sanctions is granted in part; denied in part. The subpoenas to the medical providers shall be limited to 10 years prior to the incident. The records shall be first produced to Plaintiff's counsel for a "first look." If Plaintiff's counsel believes that any records are irrelevant and should not be produced, they are directed to meet and confer with Defendant's counsel to attempt to agree to a narrower scope. If an agreement can't be reached, Plaintiff's counsel shall submit the records to JPT McDonald for an in-camera review. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Medical Examination Of Plaintiff By Defendant San Francisco Zoological Society
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lauren Meisel, esq. (415-788-2035) of Meisel, Krentsa & Burneikis for Plaintiff KIMBERLY HOM.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY's Motion To Compel Medical Examination Of Plaintiff is granted. Dr. Strassberg shall focus primarily on conducting the physical examination but may ask questions that will allow him to form an intelligent opinion of Plaintiff's injuries. See Sharaff v. Superior Court (1955) 22 Cal.2d 508. The Court does not believe that a "full medical history" is necessary for Dr. Strassberg to render an opinion but he shall be allowed to ask questions about Plaintiff's past medical conditions if they directly relate to the conditions at issue in the litigation. The conditions at issue are those listed in Exh. M to the Garrett declaration filed in support of this motion. Plaintiff's counsel reserves the right to object to Dr. Strassberg's questions but shall not terminate the examination again unless the questioning is truly oppressive. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
